analysis.core_story_summary: After defeating Kaliy, Lord Krishna and all other people of Vraj felt extremely tired. They were very hungry and thirsty also. So they did not go back to Vrindavana, but stayed on the bank of Yamuna in the night.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: Mostly suitable for broad audiences with light cultural context.

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **Drinking of Davanal (Forest Fire) — Kids (8-12):** After defeating Kaliy, Lord Krishna and all other people of Vraj felt extremely tired. They were very hungry and thirsty also. So they did not go back to Vrindavana, but stayed on the bank of Yamuna in the night. This story teaches actions have consequences, humility before higher wisdom. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.teens_13_17: **Drinking of Davanal (Forest Fire) — Teens (13-17):** After defeating Kaliy, Lord Krishna and all other people of Vraj felt extremely tired. They were very hungry and thirsty also. So they did not go back to Vrindavana, but stayed on the bank of Yamuna in the night. For teens, the key idea is actions have consequences, humility before higher wisdom. It shows how choices, discipline, and character shape outcomes.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.young_adults_18_30: **Drinking of Davanal (Forest Fire) — Young Adults:** After defeating Kaliy, Lord Krishna and all other people of Vraj felt extremely tired. They were very hungry and thirsty also. So they did not go back to Vrindavana, but stayed on the bank of Yamuna in the night. A modern takeaway is actions have consequences, humility before higher wisdom. The narrative can be read as a reflection on purpose, responsibility, and values-driven action.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.devotional_traditional: **Drinking of Davanal (Forest Fire) — Devotional/Traditional Readers:** After defeating Kaliy, Lord Krishna and all other people of Vraj felt extremely tired. They were very hungry and thirsty also. So they did not go back to Vrindavana, but stayed on the bank of Yamuna in the night. The episode highlights actions have consequences, humility before higher wisdom. It preserves reverence for divine agency while encouraging faithful practice and humility.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.global_general: **Drinking of Davanal (Forest Fire) — Global General Audience:** After defeating Kaliy, Lord Krishna and all other people of Vraj felt extremely tired. They were very hungry and thirsty also. So they did not go back to Vrindavana, but stayed on the bank of Yamuna in the night. In universal terms, it explores actions have consequences, humility before higher wisdom. Cultural names are preserved while the message is presented in accessible contemporary English.
